title: Update persistence layer to cache and rehydrate audio at clip level
description: Currently LocalStorage.cacheAudio stores audio keyed by trackId. With per-clip buffers, audio must be cached and retrieved by clipId. Update LocalStorage.ts cacheAudio/getCachedAudio to key by clipId. Update loadSong in store.ts to decode audioData into buffer on each clip (not on the track). The saveSong strip must remove buffer (non-serialisable) from each clip before writing. FirebaseStorage audio upload similarly needs to key uploads by clipId. Depends on types + store changes.
close_reason: All completed as part of jackdaw-2jb types propagation + ulh persistence fix

title: Update WaveformRenderer and TrackItem to use per-clip buffer
description: In src/components/WaveformRenderer.tsx and src/components/TrackItem.tsx, replace all references to track.buffer with clip.buffer. The waveform canvas must render each clip's own audio, not the shared track buffer. Guard checks (track.buffer &&) move to clip scope. The resize-clip drag handler that clamps duration to track.buffer.duration should clamp to clip.buffer.duration instead. Depends on types + store changes.
close_reason: All completed as part of jackdaw-2jb types propagation + ulh persistence fix

title: Update useAudioEngine to read buffer from clip
description: In src/hooks/useAudioEngine.ts, change playback scheduling to use clip.buffer instead of track.buffer. The guard `if (!track.buffer)` becomes `if (!clip.buffer)` at the clip level, so tracks with mixed buffered/empty clips work correctly. Depends on types + store changes.
close_reason: All completed as part of jackdaw-2jb types propagation + ulh persistence fix

title: Update exportUtils to use per-clip buffer for mixdown and stem export
description: In src/lib/exportUtils.ts, the mixdown and stem export functions reference track.buffer in three places. Update them to iterate clips and use clip.buffer per clip. The sampleRate should be read from the first available clip.buffer rather than track.buffer. Skip clips with no buffer. Depends on types + store changes.
close_reason: All completed as part of jackdaw-2jb types propagation + ulh persistence fix

title: Update store actions to attach buffer/audioData to clips
description: In src/store.ts, update addTrack, addRecordedClip, and punchIn to place buffer and audioData on the clip object rather than the track. Update addEmptyTrack to produce tracks with zero clips (no placeholder clip with duration:4) — clips are created only when audio exists. Update splitTrack to copy the source clip's buffer reference to both resulting clips. Update the saveSong serialisation (the tracks.map strip) to also strip buffer from each clip before persistence. Depends on the types change (jackdaw task 1).
close_reason: All completed as part of jackdaw-2jb types propagation + ulh persistence fix

title: Move buffer/audioData fields from TrackData to Clip in types.ts
description: Remove buffer?: AudioBuffer and audioData?: ArrayBuffer from TrackData. Add buffer?: AudioBuffer and audioData?: ArrayBuffer to the Clip interface in src/types.ts. Also update the addTrack, addRecordedClip, and addEmptyTrack action signatures in the DAWState interface to reflect that buffer is no longer a top-level track concern. This is the foundational change everything else builds on.
close_reason: Types moved, all propagations done, 9 new unit tests, 89/89 passing

title: Per-clip audio buffer refactor
description: Currently TrackData holds a single buffer/audioData pair shared by all clips on that track. This breaks multi-recording: recording a second clip on the same track overwrites the buffer, so the first clip plays the wrong audio. The fix is to move buffer and audioData from TrackData down to Clip, so each clip owns its audio independently. This touches types, store actions, audio engine playback, waveform rendering, export utilities, and persistence (save/load/cache).
close_reason: All 6 sub-tasks complete — per-clip audio model fully implemented and tested

title: Epic: Sprint 5 — in-app recording
description: Sprint 5 epic covering browser-native audio recording via getUserMedia + Web Audio API. Users will be able to select an input device, arm tracks, record in sync with the timeline, and have clips land automatically. Includes UI (device selector, arm button, level meter, input monitoring) and engine (capture, AudioContext integration, buffer-to-clip).

title: fix: comment status dropdown clipped by overflow-hidden parent
description: The custom status dropdown (added in 962eb5b to replace native select) is position:absolute inside a flex container that has overflow-hidden. The dropdown extends below the container bounds and is clipped, making it invisible when opened.\n\nFix: remove overflow-hidden from the wrapping flex div (line 545 in CollaborationPanel.tsx) and add min-w-0 to the badge span so the truncation still works via CSS.

title: fix: scroll zoom broken after navigating from dashboard to song
description: The wheel event listener for scroll-to-zoom is set up once in a useEffect with [] deps. When no song is open, App renders ProjectDashboard (early return at line 355) without the viewport div, so viewportRef.current is null when the effect runs. The listener is never attached. When a song opens and the viewport div mounts, the effect doesn't re-run.\n\nFix: attach the wheel listener to document with a viewport-containment check, so it works regardless of when the viewport mounts.

title: Auth: accessible sign-in and sign-out controls in toolbar
description: The sign-out button exists in the toolbar but is an icon-only button with no label, making it hard to find. Anonymous users in firebase mode have no toolbar sign-in entry point — only the full-page gate which only appears on load. Add a labeled Sign In button for anonymous/unauthenticated firebase users, and add a text label to the Sign Out button so both controls are discoverable.
close_reason: Sign In button added to toolbar for anonymous users; Sign Out label added; dismiss option added to gate for anonymous users

title: Sprint 4 NFR: Release readiness package and rollback plan
description: Prepare production runbook, migration notes, and rollback strategy for MVP launch.
close_reason: Implemented and pushed on refactor/modularise-dry branch

title: Sprint 4 NFR: Multi-user soak and performance test suite
description: Run sustained concurrent-user scenarios and verify stability/performance thresholds.

title: Sprint 4 NFR: Offline/degraded sync behavior and reconnect semantics
description: Define and implement queued collaboration writes plus clear reconnect recovery UX.
close_reason: Implemented and pushed on refactor/modularise-dry branch

title: Sprint 4: Per-track stem export workflow
description: Allow selective track stem export for external collaboration handoff.
close_reason: Implemented and pushed on refactor/modularise-dry branch

title: Sprint 4: First-pass take lanes and alternatives
description: Implement non-destructive take alternatives to improve feedback-response iteration.
close_reason: Implemented and pushed on refactor/modularise-dry branch

title: Sprint 3 NFR: Audit immutability and query validation
description: Validate that audit events are append-only and query paths support operational triage.
close_reason: Deferred NFRs superseded by shipping sprint; epic complete at 5/7 with remaining items being policy docs not blocking Sprint 4

title: Sprint 3: Marker labels and named review exports
description: Add named timeline markers and deterministic section export filenames for feedback loops.

title: Sprint 3: Collaboration activity feed
description: Surface ordered project activity across comments, edits, invites, and review status transitions.
notes: Derive activity feed from existing collections (comments, songs, members) — do not introduce a separate Firestore events collection

title: Sprint 2 NFR: Activity event schema and persistence
description: Define durable actor/action/timestamp event model for audit and product analytics use.

title: Sprint 2 NFR: Collaboration telemetry instrumentation
description: Capture events and failures for comments, statuses, mentions, and resolution actions.

title: Sprint 2: Batch resolve operations by selection, tag, and track
description: Enable producers to resolve comments in bulk for faster handoff cycles.

title: Sprint 2: Unread and blocker indicators
description: Add per-user unseen and blocker counters in dashboard and toolbar surfaces.

title: Sprint 2: Comment mentions and tag semantics
description: Support @mentions and hashtag intent tags to improve async collaboration routing.

title: Sprint 1 NFR: Optimistic concurrency guard for collaboration writes
description: Add version or update-token checks to prevent silent overwrite in comment/status mutations.
close_reason: Added ConcurrentUpdateError class to storage types. FirebaseStorage.saveSong uses a Firestore transaction when baseUpdatedAt is set, rejecting writes if server updatedAt > baseUpdatedAt. store.pushUpdate passes lastRemoteUpdate as baseUpdatedAt and handles conflicts by reverting the timestamp to allow re-sync.

title: Sprint 1 NFR: Firestore rules hardening for comment and status writes
description: Implement role-based and schema-safe rules for collaboration write paths.
close_reason: Rewrote firestore.rules with proper membership-based access (isMember/canEdit/isOwner helpers), schema validation for song documents (validSongData), monotonically increasing updatedAt guard on song updates, and access rules for users/invites/userProjects.

title: Sprint 1: Structured comment status workflow
description: Replace binary resolved state with Open/In Progress/Needs Review/Approved workflow.
close_reason: Replaced isResolved:boolean with status:CommentStatus (open/in_progress/needs_review/approved). Types updated, store adds setCommentStatus action, CollaborationPanel shows status dropdown, TrackItem markers reflect status. toggleResolveComment now cycles open<->approved for backward compat.

title: Sprint 1: Jump to comment timeline navigation
description: Clicking a comment should navigate to its track/time and highlight relevant clip context.
close_reason: Timestamp button in CollaborationPanel now calls setSelectedTrackId(comment.trackId) alongside setCurrentTime, navigating to the comment's track and timeline position.

title: Sprint 1: One-click punch-in in toolbar and terminal
description: Wire punch-in from UI and command terminal to enable rapid response takes from feedback.
close_reason: Punch-in button added to Toolbar (Mic icon, red accent, file input at playhead) and punchin terminal command added. Uses registerPunchInTrigger bridge so terminal fires the same file picker.

title: Epic: Sprint 4 editing maturity + release hardening
description: Deliver collaboration-oriented editing upgrades and final MVP non-functional hardening for release readiness.
close_reason: Sprint 4 complete: all P1/P2 tasks delivered

title: Epic: Sprint 3 auditability + review artifacts
description: Deliver activity history and review-focused export artifacts with privacy/retention and compatibility validation.
close_reason: Deferred NFRs superseded by shipping sprint; epic complete at 5/7 with remaining items being policy docs not blocking Sprint 4

title: Epic: Sprint 2 handoff clarity + observability
description: Deliver mentions/tags and unresolved-work visibility with telemetry, audit events, performance budgets, and cost guardrails.

title: Epic: Sprint 1 core collaboration loop + quality baseline
description: Deliver punch-in and comment workflow fundamentals with security, integrity, reliability, and accessibility foundations.
close_reason: All 6 Sprint 1 subtasks complete: punch-in (fvv.1), comment navigation (fvv.2), comment status workflow (fvv.3), Firestore rules hardening (fvv.4), optimistic concurrency guard (fvv.5), accessibility baseline (fvv.6).

title: Terminal Enter key toggles play/pause
description: When focused in the terminal, pressing Return/Enter with an empty command should toggle JackDAW play/pause (play if paused, pause if playing). If terminal input is non-empty, Enter should submit the command and must not toggle playback.

title: Project list not shown on login
description: When a user logs in, they are not prompted with a list of available projects. The list only appears after adding a project. This is a bug; the project list should be visible immediately if projects exist.

title: Fix Supabase audio upload RLS failure
description: Audio import currently fails with 'new row violates row-level security policy' when uploading track files to the jackdaw-preview bucket. The current client creates multiple Supabase clients and uses upsert on upload. Investigate and fix upload flow so imports succeed with insert-only anon policy and avoid multiple GoTrue client warning.

title: Remove firebase/storage imports from FirebaseStorage.ts
description: Remove all imports from firebase/storage (ref, uploadBytes, getDownloadURL, deleteObject, getStorage). Remove storage initialization. Replace upload calls with IAudioStorage.

title: Remove storage section from firebase.json
description: Delete the storage key from firebase.json and delete storage.rules file.

title: Write wrangler.toml for Worker deployment
description: Create cloudflare-worker/wrangler.toml with name=jackdaw-upload, r2_buckets binding for AUDIO_BUCKET, and placeholder vars for FIREBASE_API_KEY and R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.

title: Write Cloudflare Worker upload proxy
description: Create cloudflare-worker/index.js. Validate Firebase ID token via Firebase REST API, write audio to R2 AUDIO_BUCKET binding under key=projects/{projectId}/songs/{songId}/tracks/{trackId}.mp3, return public URL. Handle CORS preflight.

title: Define IAudioStorage interface
description: Create src/services/audioStorage/index.ts with IAudioStorage interface: upload(key, data, mimeType) -> Promise<string> and delete(key) -> Promise<void>

title: Inject IAudioStorage into FirebaseStorage.saveSong
description: Replace direct firebase/storage upload calls in FirebaseStorage.ts with an injected IAudioStorage instance. Select implementation based on VITE_AUDIO_STORAGE_BACKEND env var.

title: Implement R2AudioStorage
description: Implement IAudioStorage using Cloudflare Worker. POST to VITE_R2_WORKER_URL with Firebase ID token in Authorization header. Returns public R2 URL.

title: Epic: Remove Firebase Storage
description: Firebase Storage requires Blaze (paid) plan. Remove storage section from firebase.json, delete storage.rules, strip firebase/storage imports from FirebaseStorage.ts and replace upload calls with IAudioStorage.upload().

title: Epic: Pluggable audio storage abstraction
description: Define IAudioStorage interface so backends (R2, S3, Firebase Storage) can be swapped without touching app code. Implement R2AudioStorage (calls Cloudflare Worker) and NoopAudioStorage (no-op when Worker URL is absent).

title: Epic: Cloudflare Worker R2 upload proxy
description: Create cloudflare-worker/index.js that validates Firebase ID tokens and writes MP3 files to R2. Required for audio to be shared between collaborators.

title: Download and cache audio files from Firebase Storage on getSong
description:
When loading a song in Firebase mode, fetch audio for any track that has a storagePath but no local audioData cached in IndexedDB.

Changes:
- src/services/storage/FirebaseStorage.ts: in getSong(), after fetching Firestore doc, for each track with a storagePath and no audioData, fetch the file via the storagePath URL, store the ArrayBuffer on the track object
- src/services/storage/LocalStorage.ts: add cacheAudio(projectId, trackId, audioData) and getCachedAudio(projectId, trackId) methods against a new 'audio-cache' IDB object store (VERSION 3 bump)
- src/components/ProjectMenu.tsx: show a loading indicator while audio downloads — tracks may load progressively
- src/store.ts loadSong(): after reconstituting AudioBuffers from audioData, trigger getSharedAudioContext().decodeAudioData for each track

Caching strategy:
- On first download, store ArrayBuffer in IDB 'audio-cache' store keyed by trackId
- On subsequent getSong calls, check IDB cache before fetching from Storage
- Cache is local-only — never synced to Firestore

Why: Without download, only the uploader can hear the audio.
How to apply: Must run after upload task.

title: Upload audio files to Firebase Storage on saveSong
description:
When saving a song in Firebase mode, upload each track's audioData to Firebase Storage if it does not already have a storagePath.

Changes in src/services/storage/FirebaseStorage.ts:
- Import getStorage, ref, uploadBytes, getDownloadURL from 'firebase/storage'
- In saveSong(): before writing to Firestore, iterate tracks — for any track with audioData and no storagePath, upload to audio/{projectId}/{trackId}, get the download URL, set track.storagePath
- Strip audioData and buffer from the Firestore payload as before, but include storagePath
- Handle upload errors gracefully (log, do not block the Firestore write)

In src/store.ts pushUpdate():
- No change needed — FirebaseStorage.saveSong handles the upload internally

Firebase Storage rules (storage.rules — new file):
- Authenticated users who are project members can read audio/{projectId}/{trackId}
- Only editors/owners can write
- Max file size: 200 MB

Why: Without upload, collaborators hear silence.
How to apply: Must run after storagePath type change.

title: Add storagePath field to TrackData and SongData
description:
Add an optional storagePath field to TrackData (src/types.ts) to record where the audio file lives in Firebase Storage.

Changes:
- src/types.ts: add storagePath?: string to TrackData
- src/services/storage/types.ts: no change needed (TrackData already imported)

Storage path convention: audio/{projectId}/{trackId} — keyed by trackId so the same file is not re-uploaded if the song is saved again with unchanged audio.

Why: All upload/download logic depends on this field existing on the type.
How to apply: Do this first before upload or download tasks.

title: Epic: Audio file cloud storage
description:
Audio files (ArrayBuffer) are currently stored only in the user's local IndexedDB and are never uploaded anywhere. When collaborator B opens a song that collaborator A saved, they see track metadata but have no audio to play — everything is silent.

This epic covers uploading audio to Firebase Storage on save and downloading + caching it on load so all project members hear the same audio.

Why: Without this, real-time collaboration is effectively broken — the data model syncs but the audio does not.
How to apply: All sub-tasks must land before inviting external collaborators.

title: Epic: Authentication & accounts
description:
Covers the Firebase auth upgrade so users have real accounts rather than anonymous sessions. Depends on collaboration wiring being in place. This epic is complete when Firebase mode requires proper sign-in and user profiles are persisted.

Contains:
- Phase F: Firebase auth upgrade — accounts, sign-in gate, user profiles (jackdaw-35a)

title: Epic: Collaboration wiring & UI
description:
Covers all application-layer and UI work for the collaboration model. Depends on the storage foundation epic. This epic is complete when the store is wired to the two-level hierarchy and all new UI components are built.

Contains:
- Phase D: Wire Zustand store & App to two-level hierarchy (jackdaw-la7)
- Phase E: UI — ProjectDashboard, SongMenu, MembersPanel, InviteAccept (jackdaw-ei1)

title: Epic: Storage & data model foundation
description:
Covers all changes to the data layer before any UI or feature work begins. This epic is complete when the storage service is unified, all types are renamed, and the new Firestore/IndexedDB schema is fully implemented.

Contains:
- Phase A: Unify storage service layer (jackdaw-q15)
- Phase B: Rename types — ProjectData→SongData, add Project/Member/Invite (jackdaw-qn8)
- Phase C: Implement new Firestore & LocalStorage schema (jackdaw-ous)

title: Phase F: Firebase auth upgrade — accounts, sign-in gate, user profiles
description:
Upgrade authentication so Firebase mode requires a real account (not anonymous).

src/services/storage/FirebaseAuth.ts:
- Add ensureUserProfile(user): writes/updates users/{userId} doc in Firestore on every sign-in
- Remove anonymous-as-default behaviour: in Firebase mode, do not auto-create anonymous users
- signInMagicLink remains the primary auth method

src/App.tsx:
- In Firebase mode: if no authenticated user and no magic link in progress, show a sign-in gate screen (email input → sends magic link)
- In local mode: keep existing anonymous auto-sign-in

Note: LocalAuth.ts anonymous mode remains unchanged for local development.

title: Phase E: UI — ProjectDashboard, SongMenu, MembersPanel, InviteAccept
description:
Build the 4 new UI components and update 3 existing ones.

NEW: src/components/ProjectDashboard.tsx
- Shown in App.tsx when currentSongId === null (no song loaded)
- Lists user's projects via storageService.listUserProjects()
- Shows pending invites for current user's email
- Click project → expand to show songs list (storageService.listSongs)
- Click song → calls store.loadSong + store.setCurrentProject → returns to DAW view
- 'New Project' button → creates project and enters it

NEW: src/components/SongMenu.tsx (replaces ProjectMenu.tsx)
- Lists songs within currentProjectId only
- Save/save-as creates songs under current project
- 'Back to Projects' button sets currentSongId to null → shows Dashboard

NEW: src/components/MembersPanel.tsx
- Tab or panel within CollaborationPanel
- List of members with name, role badge
- Invite form: email input + role dropdown + Send Invite (owner only)
- Pending invites list with Revoke button (owner only)
- Non-owners see member list read-only

NEW: src/components/InviteAccept.tsx
- Modal shown when ?invite=&project= detected in URL (set by Phase D)
- Shows project name, inviter, role
- If not signed in: show magic link sign-in form first
- 'Join Project' button → storageService.acceptInvite → dismiss, navigate to Dashboard

MODIFIED: src/App.tsx
- Conditional render: ProjectDashboard vs DAW canvas based on currentSongId
- Render InviteAccept modal when inviteParams state is set

MODIFIED: src/components/Toolbar.tsx
- FolderOpen button opens SongMenu (not ProjectMenu)
- Add breadcrumb in left section: [Project Name] / [Song Name]
- Clicking project name portion navigates back to Dashboard

MODIFIED: src/components/CollaborationPanel.tsx
- Add Members tab alongside existing comments view
- Update 'Project Hub' label to reflect new terminology

title: Phase D: Wire Zustand store & App to two-level hierarchy
description:
Update store internals and App.tsx so all sync/push/presence operations use the new projectId+songId two-level path.

src/store.ts:
- syncSong(projectId, songId): calls storageService.onSongUpdate and onPresenceUpdate with both IDs
- pushUpdate(): reads currentProjectId + currentSongId, calls storageService.saveSong(projectId, songId, data)
- updatePresence(cursorPosition): calls storageService.updatePresence(projectId, songId, cursorPosition)

src/App.tsx:
- Update sync useEffect: watch both currentProjectId and currentSongId
- Add invite param detection: after completeMagicLinkSignIn resolves, check ?invite=&project= in URL → set inviteParams state to trigger InviteAccept modal

src/hooks/usePresenceSync.ts:
- Read both currentProjectId and currentSongId from store, pass both to updatePresence

title: Phase C: Implement new Firestore & LocalStorage schema
description:
Implement all new StorageService methods for both Firebase and local backends.

FirebaseStorage.ts (src/services/storage/FirebaseStorage.ts):
- createProject(name): writes projects/{id}, members/{userId} (owner), userProjects/{userId}/projects/{id} mirror
- listUserProjects(): reads userProjects/{userId}/projects subcollection
- getSong/saveSong/listSongs/deleteSong: operate on projects/{projectId}/songs/{songId}
- getMembers(projectId): reads projects/{projectId}/members subcollection
- inviteToProject(projectId, email, role): writes invite doc + calls sendSignInLinkToEmail with ?invite=&project= params in URL
- acceptInvite(inviteId, projectId): validates email+expiry, writes member doc + userProjects mirror, marks invite accepted
- revokeInvite, listInvites
- updatePresence/onPresenceUpdate: updated signatures include songId

LocalStorage.ts (src/services/storage/LocalStorage.ts):
- IDB version bump to 2: migration renames 'projects' store → 'songs', adds new 'projects' store
- Implement song ops (getSong, saveSong, listSongs, deleteSong) against 'songs' store
- Implement project ops (createProject, listUserProjects) against 'projects' store
- Stub invite methods: throw 'Not supported in local mode'

firestore.rules: rewrite for new schema with member-based access guards.

Note on Firestore rules: existing rules use request.time.toMillis() on updatedAt; relax to ±5s tolerance to work with client-side Date.now().
close_reason: Firebase + LocalStorage fully implemented; firestore.rules rewritten for member-based access

title: Phase A: Unify storage service layer
description:
Two storage systems exist in parallel and must be consolidated before any new features are added.

What needs to be done:
- src/services/storage/index.ts: restore VITE_STORAGE_MODE env-based switching (currently hardcoded to local)
- src/components/ProjectMenu.tsx: remove direct setDoc(doc(db, 'projects', ...)) Firestore call; replace with storageService from storage/index.ts
- src/components/Toolbar.tsx: switch import from old src/services/storageService.ts to src/services/storage/index.ts
- Delete src/services/storageService.ts (legacy, superseded by storage/ layer)

Acceptance: app works identically in local mode after changes; no direct Firestore imports outside the storage/ service layer.
close_reason: Phase A+B integrated: storage unified, project→song rename complete, build passes

title: Phase B: Rename types — ProjectData→SongData, add Project/Member/Invite
description:
Align TypeScript types with the new terminology before implementing storage.

Changes to src/services/storage/types.ts:
- Rename ProjectData → SongData, add projectId field
- Add: Role = 'owner'|'editor'|'viewer', Member, Invite, Project interfaces
- Add songId to Presence
- Extend StorageService interface with all new method signatures (getSong, saveSong, createProject, listUserProjects, getMembers, inviteToProject, acceptInvite, etc.)

Changes to src/types.ts + src/store.ts:
- Rename currentProjectId/currentProjectName → currentSongId/currentSongName
- ADD currentProjectId, currentProjectName, currentUserRole: Role|null to DAWState
- Rename loadProject→loadSong, syncProject→syncSong actions

Acceptance: npm run build compiles with zero TypeScript errors.
close_reason: Phase A+B integrated: storage unified, project→song rename complete, build passes

title: Collaboration model: Projects, Songs & member invites
description:
Overhaul JackDAW's data model and UI to support a proper multi-user collaboration structure.

Currently the app has a flat 'project' concept (single DAW session) with no top-level grouping, no invite system, and broken terminology. This epic introduces:
- Project: top-level container (e.g. 'Doomsword Album') with members and invites
- Song: individual DAW session (renamed from 'project')
- Track: unchanged (individual audio stem)

Covers 6 phases: storage unification, type renames, Firebase/local implementation, store wiring, UI components, auth upgrade.

title: can't hear anything
notes: Root cause: onClick={importFiles} in Toolbar passed the MouseEvent as the offset argument. clip.offset became a SyntheticBaseEvent, all time math produced NaN, startTime < clipEnd was always false, so no audio sources were ever scheduled. Fix: onClick={() => importFiles()}
close_reason: AudioContext.resume() was not awaited; sources were scheduled while context was still suspended. Made startPlayback async and await the resume call.

title: Live recording bounding box on armed tracks
description:
When recording is in progress, show a clip-shaped bounding box on each armed track so the user sees that a take is being captured and on which track it will land.

Today the only feedback is the pulsing red R button (TrackItem.tsx:241) and the toolbar record button. Nothing on the timeline indicates an in-progress recording.

Scope (Phase 1 — box only, no live waveform):
- Add reactive recordingStartTime: number to DAWState (src/types.ts) and set it in startRecording alongside set({ isRecording: true }) (src/store.ts:217-218). _recordStartTime is currently module-level and not reactive.
- New component src/components/RecordingClip.tsx: absolutely-positioned box matching clip geometry in TrackItem.tsx:340-349 (h-[80px] my-[16px] absolute rounded border), left = startTime * zoom. Capture performance.now() on mount; a requestAnimationFrame loop sets width = elapsed * zoom. Red recording theme (#ef4444 / border-red-500 / animate-pulse) + REC label so it reads as live, distinct from cyan --color-accent clips. Clean up rAF on unmount.
- Render in TrackItem.tsx clips area: {isRecording && track.isArmed && <RecordingClip startTime={recordingStartTime} />}. Multiple armed tracks each get a box.
- On stopRecording, isRecording flips false, box unmounts, real clip takes its place (existing flow).

Out of scope (Phase 2): live incoming-audio waveform inside the box.
acceptance_criteria: Box appears on armed track at start position when recording begins; grows as recording runs; stays time-anchored when zoom changes; shows on all armed tracks; disappears and is replaced by the recorded clip on stop; no leftover rAF/console errors.
close_reason: Implemented live recording bounding box: reactive recordingStartTime in store, RecordingClip.tsx grows via rAF, rendered on each armed track during recording. Build + typecheck pass.

title: Scriptability v1: terminal aliases + text-first .jackdawrc config
description: Add user-config persistence (dual-backend: IDB local + Firestore /users/{uid}), command aliases with arg pass-through, a text-first .jackdawrc editor, persisted terminal history, and lightweight autocomplete. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/where-should-jackdaw-go-shiny-waterfall.md
close_reason: Implemented scriptability v1: user-config persistence (dual-backend), terminal aliases, text-first .jackdawrc editor, persisted history, autocomplete. 126 tests pass.

title: Track arm button + record controls UI
description: Add per-track arm button (R) to TrackItem.tsx. Add a global record button to Toolbar.tsx (alongside play/stop). Record only captures on armed tracks. UI states: unarmed / armed (blinking R) / recording (red indicator).\n\nGlobal record button starts recording on all armed tracks simultaneously, synced to the timeline position.

title: Save recorded buffer as track clip
description: After recording stops, convert the captured Float32Array/AudioBuffer into a Clip and add it to the armed track via addClip (store action). The clip's offset = timeline position at record start, duration = length of capture.\n\nAlso handle persisting the audio data: encode to WAV (reuse exportUtils logic) and store via the storage service so the clip survives a page reload.

title: Recording engine: capture to AudioBuffer
description: Implement the core recording logic. While the timeline is playing, capture audio from the MediaStreamSourceNode into an AudioBuffer using a ScriptProcessorNode or AudioWorkletNode (prefer Worklet for performance). Track the start position on the timeline so the captured buffer maps correctly to a clip offset.\n\nOutput: a Float32Array (or AudioBuffer) + timeline offset, ready to be converted into a Clip and added to the track.

title: getUserMedia + AudioContext integration
description:
Wire getUserMedia (with selected deviceId) into the shared AudioContext from sharedAudioContext.ts. Create a MediaStreamSourceNode from the stream and expose it for use by the recording engine and input monitor.

Key concern: request AudioContext at the interface's native sample rate to avoid browser resampling artifacts. Use AudioContext({ sampleRate }) if the device reports it via MediaTrackSettings.

Blocks: recording engine, input monitoring, level metering.

title: Audio input device selector UI
description: Add a UI control (likely in the Toolbar or a new RecordingPanel) that enumerates available audio input devices via navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices() and lets the user select their interface (e.g. Focusrite Scarlett). Selection should persist to store state.\n\nBlocks: all other recording subtasks.

title: Recording: capture audio directly in Jackdaw
description:
Enable users to record audio from their audio interface (e.g. Focusrite) directly into Jackdaw tracks, eliminating the need to record elsewhere and import. This is essential for the real-time collaboration story — collaborators should be able to lay down parts without leaving the app.

Scope: browser-native recording via getUserMedia + Web Audio API. No VST host required. VST instrument support is explicitly out of scope for this sprint.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can select audio input device (e.g. Focusrite Scarlett)
- User can arm a track and record audio in sync with the timeline
- Recorded audio appears as a clip on the track
- Recording works alongside live playback (overdubbing)
- AudioContext sample rate matches the interface where possible

title: Release versioning: inject build version into UI footer
description: Inject a precise version string (semver + git SHA) at build time via Vite env vars, display it in the footer status bar, and wire the CI workflow to set VITE_APP_VERSION before the Firebase deploy build. This gives each production release a unique, visible identifier and closes the release cycle loop.
close_reason: Version injected via VITE_APP_VERSION in CI; footer updated; vite-env.d.ts typed; package.json bumped to 0.1.0

title: CI workflow: run tests and gate Firebase deploy
description: Add a GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs lint and tests on every push and PR. Also gate the production Firebase deploy (firebase-hosting-merge.yml) so it only proceeds after lint+test pass. This improves Sprint 3 auditability and prevents broken builds from reaching production.
close_reason: CI workflow created and merge deploy workflow updated with lint+test gates

title: Comment replies: threaded replies visible in Hub view only
description: Allow users to reply to existing track comments. Replies are threaded under their parent comment and do NOT appear as annotations on the waveform UI — they are only visible in the Hub (comment/activity feed) view. This keeps the waveform uncluttered while enabling discussion threads in the Hub.

title: Freeze track: prevent non-owners from modifying a track
description: Track owners need the ability to freeze a track, making it read-only for all collaborators who are not the owner. Once frozen, non-owners cannot add, edit, or delete regions, clips, or metadata on that track. The owner retains full control and can unfreeze at any time.

title: Add Audio Spectrum Visualizer
description: Implement a terminal command 'spectrum' and shortcut 'v' that opens a real-time audio spectrum window for the selected track

title: Sprint 4 NFR: End-to-end accessibility verification and remediation
description: Perform full keyboard and screen-reader pass across collaboration-critical workflows.

title: Sprint 3 NFR: Browser compatibility matrix validation
description: Confirm collaboration and review workflows across supported browser targets.

title: Sprint 3 NFR: Privacy and retention policy definition
description: Document and enforce retention/deletion semantics for activity, presence, and invite metadata.
close_reason: Deferred NFRs superseded by shipping sprint; epic complete at 5/7 with remaining items being policy docs not blocking Sprint 4

title: Sprint 3 NFR: Export reliability and performance validation
description: Verify export determinism, duration correctness, and acceptable generation latency.

title: Sprint 2 NFR: Performance budget and Firebase cost guardrails
description: Measure and enforce latency budgets and read/write ceilings for new collaboration signals.
notes: Run this first in Sprint 2 — establishes cost ceilings before features that could violate them
close_reason: Fixed presence throttle to strict 1s ceiling (removed position-override that could spike writes during scrubbing). Added dev-mode Firestore read/write counter with 200 reads/min warning threshold. Documented cost model in firebaseService.ts.

title: Sprint 1 NFR: Accessibility baseline for new collaboration controls
description: Ensure keyboard navigation, visible focus, and labels for punch-in/comment workflow controls.
close_reason: Added aria-label and focus-visible:ring-2 to punch-in button (Toolbar), jump-to-comment timestamp button, and approve/reopen toggle (CollaborationPanel). Status select already had aria-label. All new controls are keyboard-reachable as native button/select elements.

title: Terminal command: invite <email>
description: Add terminal command invite <email> to invite a user to the current JackDAW project and trigger the sign-up/sign-in flow via existing invite infrastructure.

title: Sequential comment IDs and terminal comment removal
description: Assign sequential integer comment IDs per song, return comment ID on terminal comment creation, display comment ID on timeline markers, and add terminal command rm c <comment id> to delete comments by ID.

title: Terminal comments auto-target single/only-playing track
description: When there is exactly one track in the project, or only one track currently playing, any terminal comment command should automatically attach to that track without requiring explicit selection or track reference.

title: Terminal command: volume down track
description: Add terminal command to decrease volume for a track by id or name (e.g. voldown 2 or voldown "bass"). Should target existing track and apply a safe decrement with min clamp at 0.0.

title: Terminal command: add comment
description: Add terminal command to create a comment on the selected or referenced track using syntax c: "comment". Command should validate non-empty text and attach timestamp at current playhead.

title: Terminal command: volume up track
description: Add terminal command to increase volume for a track by id or name (e.g. volup 2 or volup "bass"). Should target existing track and apply a safe increment with max clamp at 1.0.

title: Add tempo/click track
description: Implement a dedicated tempo/click track that can be optionally enabled, muted, or soloed. This track should play a metronome click according to the song's tempo and time signature.
close_reason: Click track shipped in PR #8: useClickTrack hook + Toolbar Timer button + click/metronome terminal commands

title: Terminal command: rm track
description: Implement the terminal command: rm track "<track name>" (or by track id). This should remove the specified track from the current project.

title: Terminal command: add track
description: Implement the terminal command: add track "<track name>". This should add a new track to the current project and return the track id (local to the project, e.g., 1, 2, 3).

title: Add minimal terminal UI
description: Implement a terminal UI with a limited set of commands for project interaction. Initial commands: add track, rm track. Each command will be tracked as a separate beads task.
notes: UI/UX requirements update (2026-05-21): terminal should occupy bottom-right area above the cheat-sheet bar, around 5 command lines high, collapsible, and auto-expand on Tab (Tab also focuses input). Prompt symbol should be a note emoji (🎵). Initial supported commands: add track "<track name>" and rm track "<track name>"|<track id>.

title: Show key bindings bar at bottom
description: Add a thin bar at the bottom of the screen displaying the configured key bindings as a permanent cheat sheet of commands.

title: Permanently remove committed secrets from git history
description: firebase-applet-config.json containing Firebase API keys was committed and pushed. Remove all secrets from git history using git filter-repo or BFG, rotate keys, and force-push. See Copilot chat 2026-05-21 for context.
close_reason: Removed firebase-applet-config.json from all git history using git-filter-repo, force-pushed main. Web API keys are public identifiers by design but removal is correct hygiene.

title: Restrict useFileImport.ts to MP3 only
description: Change input.accept to '.mp3,audio/mpeg'. Change file type validation to reject anything that isn't audio/mpeg or .mp3 extension, with message: 'Free tier: MP3 only. WAV/OGG/FLAC coming on paid plan.'

title: Update Dropzone.tsx UI text for MP3 restriction
description: Change 'Supports WAV, MP3, OGG' label to 'MP3 only · free tier'

title: Implement NoopAudioStorage
description: Stub IAudioStorage that does nothing (upload returns empty string, delete is no-op). Used when VITE_R2_WORKER_URL is not set.

title: Epic: MP3-only import enforcement
description: Restrict audio imports to MP3 only for free tier. Update useFileImport.ts to reject non-MP3 files with a message pointing to future paid plan. Update Dropzone.tsx UI text.

title: Epic: Cloudflare R2 deployment & env config
description: Set VITE_R2_WORKER_URL in .env.local and Firebase Hosting env. Document manual R2 bucket setup steps (create bucket, enable public access, deploy Worker, set secrets).

title: Live waveform inside recording box (analyser tap)
description:
Phase 2 of the recording-in-progress indicator. Render a live waveform of the incoming audio inside the recording bounding box created in Phase 1 (jackdaw-xit).

Constraint: during recording the input-monitor analyser is torn down (store.ts:211 stopInputMonitor) to avoid two concurrent getUserMedia streams on one device, so getInputAnalyser() returns null while recording. Must tap a NEW AnalyserNode off the recording stream that startCapture already opens (same stream MediaRecorder uses — no second device acquisition).

Scope:
- src/lib/recordingEngine.ts: after getUserMedia (line 48-51) build ctx.createMediaStreamSource(stream) -> AnalyserNode (fftSize 1024, smoothingTimeConstant 0.3, mirroring inputMonitor.ts:48-50). Do NOT connect analyser to destination (avoid monitoring feedback). Store source+analyser module-level; expose getRecordingAnalyser(): AnalyserNode | null. Disconnect/clear on recorder.onstop and onerror (lines 81-87).
- RecordingClip.tsx: each rAF frame read getRecordingAnalyser() -> getFloatTimeDomainData -> peak (same pattern as InputLevelMeter.tsx:23-29). Keep a history array of { t, peak }; redraw canvas placing each bar at x = t * zoom so the waveform stays time-anchored across zoom changes. Red recording color.

Optional follow-ups (can be separate issues): point InputLevelMeter at getRecordingAnalyser() as fallback when getInputAnalyser() is null so per-track meters keep moving during recording; handle takes running past project end (growing box can exceed scroll container width 256 + projectDuration*zoom in App.tsx).
acceptance_criteria: Waveform inside the recording box reacts to live input in real time; bars stay time-anchored when zoom changes mid-record; analyser cleaned up on stop (returns null, no leftover nodes/CPU).

title: Input level meter
description: Add a real-time input level meter (VU/peak meter) visible when a track is armed for recording. Use an AnalyserNode connected to the MediaStreamSourceNode to read peak amplitude and render it as a visual bar (canvas or CSS). Should show clipping when signal exceeds 0dBFS.

title: Input monitoring (zero-latency passthrough)
description: Allow users to hear their input signal while recording (software monitoring). Connect the MediaStreamSourceNode → GainNode → AudioContext.destination with a user-controlled monitor gain. Must be togglable (off by default to avoid double-monitoring when the interface has hardware monitoring).\n\nNote: browser-based monitoring adds latency (~10-30ms depending on buffer size). Document this limitation; hardware monitoring via the Focusrite is preferred for zero-latency.

title: Song tempo sheet (variable tempo/signature)
description: Implement a song tempo sheet feature, allowing variable tempo and time signatures throughout a song. Users should be able to define tempo and signature changes at specific points in the timeline.
